summary: Iteration 6: Tested Super Admin enhancements - role-based login redirect, Doctors page filters and edit functionality, PUT /api/users/{id} endpoint, and Staff section in clinic details. All 13 backend tests pass (100%). Frontend UI fully verified.

ui_bugs.issue:
HTML nesting warning:
(Badge) cannot be a descendant of
ui_bugs.note: Does not affect functionality - console warning only

features: Uses getRedirectPath for role-based redirect after login

passed_tests: Backend: Super admin login returns role=super_admin correctly
Backend: Doctor login returns role=doctor correctly
Backend: PUT /api/users/{id} - Super admin can update user consultation fee
Backend: PUT /api/users/{id} - Update multiple fields (name, specialty, fee, phone)
Backend: PUT /api/users/{id} - Non-super_admin denied (403)
Backend: PUT /api/users/{id} - Non-existent user returns 404
Backend: PUT /api/users/{id} - Empty payload returns 400
Backend: GET /api/users - Returns doctors with specialty data
Backend: GET /api/companies - Returns companies for filter dropdown
Backend: Users have company_id for clinic filtering
Backend: GET /api/companies/{id}/staff - Returns staff list
Backend: Staff data includes consultation_fee field
Backend: Doctor consultation fee update via staff endpoint works
Frontend: Super Admin login redirects to /super-admin
Frontend: Doctor login redirects to /dashboard
Frontend: Doctors page shows 12 doctors with filters
Frontend: Specialty filter dropdown works
Frontend: Company filter dropdown works
Frontend: Doctor edit dialog opens with all fields
Frontend: Clinic detail page loads Staff tab
Frontend: Staff tab shows consultation fees
Frontend: Staff edit dialog opens with consultation fee field
action_items:
MINOR: Fix HTML nesting warning - Move Badge outside of

tag in DoctorsPage.js line 163-165
critical_code_review_comments: getRedirectPath function correctly handles role-based routing
PUT /api/users/{id} properly restricts to super_admin only
All filters working client-side - no API filtering needed
Staff edit uses same PUT /api/users/{id} endpoint
context_for_next_testing_agent: Role-based redirect fully working: super_admin->/super-admin, company_admin->/clinic-admin, others->/dashboard. Doctors page has 3 filters (search, specialty, company) all working client-side. Edit doctor dialog updates via PUT /api/users/{id} (super_admin only). Staff section in clinic details shows consultation fee badges and has edit button that opens dialog with fee input. All features use same PUT endpoint.

doctors_page.specialty_filter: WORKING - dropdown with cardiology, pediatrics, etc

clinic_staff_section.consultation_fee_display: WORKING - green badge showing fee in SYP

put_users_endpoint.validation: empty payload returns 400, non-existent user returns 404
